feature_tested: ZARO Guardian Safety Layer — Red Phoenix (Military-grade security with 5 Guardian subsystems)
summary: Comprehensive testing of ZARO Guardian Safety Layer. All 16 backend API tests passed (100%). Frontend ZARO Guardian panel fully functional with all key elements verified including 5 Guardian subsystems, DEFCON badge, Security Score, Red Phoenix Alerts, and Security Posture components.

endpoints: GET /api/zaro/status - returns all 5 guardians (SENTINEL, AEGIS, CERBERUS, HEIMDALL, VALKYRIE) as active with stats
GET /api/zaro/scan - Red Phoenix operational threat scan returning DEFCON level 2 CRITICAL and alerts
POST /api/zaro/sentinel/scan - detects SQL injection (UNION SELECT password FROM admin)
POST /api/zaro/sentinel/scan - returns clean:true for safe payload (Hello world)
POST /api/zaro/aegis/scan-pii - detects phone, email, SSN in text
POST /api/zaro/aegis/scan-pii - returns redacted text with [REDACTED_*] markers
POST /api/zaro/aegis/scan-pii - detects credit card numbers (high severity)
POST /api/zaro/aegis/scan-pii - returns no findings for clean text
GET /api/zaro/heimdall/observability - returns request tracking metrics (requests_5min, errors_5min, avg_response_ms, top_endpoints)
GET /api/zaro/incidents - returns incident list with count
GET /api/zaro/security-posture - returns security score (90), grade (A), and 5 components
ZARO Sentinel scanning wired into security middleware (lines 389-395 in server.py)
ZARO Heimdall tracking records request duration for all /api/* calls (line 424 in server.py)

verified_elements: DEFCON badge with level 2 and CRITICAL label (orange color)
guardian-sentinel - SENTINEL card with blocked IPs and strikes (data-testid)
guardian-aegis - AEGIS card with PII patterns and fields (data-testid)
guardian-cerberus - CERBERUS card with lockouts and failures (data-testid)
guardian-heimdall - HEIMDALL card with tracked requests and anomalies (data-testid)
guardian-valkyrie - VALKYRIE card with open incidents (data-testid)
Pulsing status indicators on all Guardian cards
phoenix-alerts - Red Phoenix Alerts section with CRITICAL severity badge (data-testid)
ui_features_verified: ZARO Guardian panel loads from sidebar under Analytics & AI
DEFCON level 2 badge with orange color coding (CRITICAL)
Security Score 90 and Grade A displayed prominently
5 Guardian subsystem cards with pulsing green status indicators
Guardian cards show real-time stats (blocked IPs, PII patterns, lockouts, tracked requests, incidents)
Security Posture shows 5 components with color-coded status
Red Phoenix Alerts section shows 1 CRITICAL alert (inventory stockout)
Alert shows corrective action and escalation targets
Security Incidents section expandable (shows 'No security incidents recorded')

seed_data_creation: No seed data created - tests used existing MongoDB collections
context_for_next_testing_agent: ZARO Guardian Safety Layer is fully functional. All 5 Guardian subsystems (SENTINEL, AEGIS, CERBERUS, HEIMDALL, VALKYRIE) are active and integrated into the security middleware. SENTINEL scans all incoming /api/* requests for attack signatures (SQL injection, XSS, path traversal, command injection, XXE). AEGIS detects and redacts PII (SSN, credit card, email, phone, IP). CERBERUS tracks failed logins with 5-attempt lockout. HEIMDALL tracks request duration and detects anomalies. VALKYRIE manages security incidents. Red Phoenix scans operational data for food safety violations, financial threshold breaches, inventory stockouts, compliance backlogs, and security incidents. Current DEFCON level is 2 (CRITICAL) due to 59 ingredients at ZERO stock. Security Score is 90 Grade A. Toast POS and QuickBooks integrations remain MOCKED with demo/sandbox data.
notes: All 16 backend tests passed. Frontend ZARO Guardian panel fully verified with Playwright automation. The panel correctly displays all 5 Guardian subsystems with pulsing status indicators, DEFCON level badge with color coding, Security Score and Grade, Security Posture components, Red Phoenix Alerts with severity badges and corrective actions, escalation targets, and expandable Security Incidents section. The middleware integration is LIVE — Sentinel scans all incoming /api/* requests for attack signatures, and Heimdall tracks request duration for observability.
